vision_statement: Decifer is a cloud-hosted multi-user trading bot that friends and family can connect to via a web interface, linking their own broker accounts and managing their own risk profiles, built on a profitable and reliable algorithmic core that is proven on paper before going live.
current_stage_assessment: Decifer is formally in the paper_trading_single_account stage but has recently shipped Phase E multi-account position aggregation infrastructure, suggesting development is partially running ahead of the stage sequence. The immediate priority must be proving the signal and risk engine is profitable and stable in paper trading before any live money or multi-user complexity is layered on. The test suite is reporting 0% pass rate — this is a critical blocker that must be resolved before any forward progress is trustworthy.
executive_summary: Decifer has a strong and ambitious technical foundation — 19,477 lines across 26 modules, a 9-dimension signal engine, six shipped features, and a clear multi-stage roadmap — but is currently in a fragile state. The test suite is showing 0 passed / 0 failed / 0 errors, which almost certainly indicates a collection or runner failure rather than clean green tests, and represents the single most dangerous signal in the project right now. The active backlog contains 50 features, many of which are duplicated across the active and backlog lists (Per-Account Config Profiles, AccountDecisionAgent, Connection Manager, etc.), creating confusion about what is actually sequenced. The feature pipeline is partially mis-sequenced: multi-account infrastructure (Phase E/MA1) is being built before the core single-account paper trading loop is validated as profitable. Research findings are strong and well-grounded, pointing clearly toward IC-weighted dynamic signal scoring and fractional Kelly sizing as the highest-leverage signal and risk improvements, but these are sitting in the active list without clear prioritisation relative to the stability work needed first.

product_roadmap.goal: Restore a fully green, trustworthy test suite so that every subsequent code change can be validated with confidence.
product_roadmap.rationale: The test suite is reporting 0 passed, 0 failed, 0 errors — this is almost certainly a runner or collection failure, not a real result. With 32 test files and 942 tests referenced in recent commits, this is a silent risk: broken tests mean broken feedback loops, and every feature shipped without tests is unvalidated. Nothing else on the roadmap is trustworthy until this is fixed.
features: Diagnose and fix test runner / collection failure causing 0 results
Restore all 942 tests to passing (or explicitly mark known failures with reasons)
Confirm agents JSON parse error handling is tested and correct
Confirm data_collector network error fallback paths are tested
Confirm orders.py IBKR connection failure paths are tested
Add CI enforcement: test suite must pass before any merge
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: pytest reports a concrete pass count >= 900, zero unexplained errors, and the runner itself is confirmed reliable across clean environments.

product_roadmap.goal: Prove the single-account paper trading loop is profitable, stable, and observable over at least 30 trading days.
product_roadmap.rationale: This is the gate condition for the entire roadmap. No live money, no multi-user complexity, and no cloud deployment should happen until the core signal-to-order pipeline is demonstrably profitable on paper. The signal engine, risk engine, and execution loop must be solid before anything else is built on top of them.
features: Extract Signal Objects from Brain Loop (B P1) — separate signal generation from execution, typed Signal objects
Z-Score Normalised Signal Composite (B P1) — normalise each dimension before compositing
Multi-Timeframe Signal Confirmation (B P1) — daily/4H/1H alignment before entry
Regime-Gated Momentum / Mean-Reversion Signal Router (B P1) — route signals by detected regime
Regime-Conditional Signal Weighting (B P1) — VIX-proxy regime detector, momentum vs mean-reversion gate
ATR Volatility-Targeting Position Size Layer (B P2) — volatility-targeting formula for position sizing
Drawdown Brake — Tiered Equity Curve Circuit Breaker (B P2) — three-tier drawdown brake
VIX-Rank Adaptive Fractional Kelly Sizer (B P2) — replace static Kelly fraction with dynamic VIX-scaled version
Dual-Tranche Exit Strategy (B P2) — split positions into two tranches on entry
Signal Decay Monitor & Freshness Dashboard (B P1) — weekly IC rolling health check per dimension
Alpha Decay Chart — Per-Trade Forward Return Distribution Tracker (B P1)
Walk-Forward Backtesting (B P2) — rolling 6mo train / 1mo test windows
Slippage & Execution Cost Model for Backtester (B P2)
Monte Carlo Stress Testing (B P2) — 1000x shuffle of backtest trade sequences
Paper trading observation period: minimum 30 trading days, log all trades with rationale
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: 30+ trading days of paper trading showing positive expectancy (positive net P&L, Sharpe > 0.5), all signal dimensions showing positive or neutral IC, drawdown brake triggered and recovered at least once in simulation, walk-forward backtest completed without overfitting flags.

product_roadmap.goal: Replace the static equal-weight signal composite with IC-weighted dynamic scoring and add the highest-conviction new signal dimensions.
product_roadmap.rationale: Research findings consistently show IC-weighted dynamic signal models substantially outperform equal-weight static models. This phase should happen after the core loop is stable (Phase 1) but before live money, because changing signal weights after going live introduces unnecessary risk. New signal dimensions (options flow, insider buying, sentiment) add diversified alpha sources with research backing.
features: IC-Weighted Dynamic Signal Scoring (C P2) — rolling IC-weighted composite replacing equal-weight
Options Flow Proxy Signal via Put/Call Ratio (B P1)
Unusual Options Activity (UOA) Signal Dimension (B P1)
SEC Form 4 Insider Cluster Buying Dimension (B P2)
Claude Sentiment Scoring Pipeline (B P2) — Yahoo RSS + Finviz via Claude as 10th dimension
News Sentiment Regression Scorer via FinBERT (B P2)
Signal Correlation De-Duplication Guard (C P2) — prevent double-counting correlated dimensions
Signal Validation with Alphalens (C P2) — validate each dimension's IC scores and forward returns
Alpha Decay Monitor — Per-Signal IC Rolling Health Check (C P2)
Anchored VWAP Entry Gate (B P2) — binary gate before entry
Multi-Timeframe Momentum Confirmation Filter (B P2)
Order Flow Imbalance Detection (B P2)
Time-of-Day Seasonality Filter (B P2)
Meta-Labeling Secondary Classifier (B P2)
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: IC-weighted composite demonstrably outperforms equal-weight composite in walk-forward backtest. All new signal dimensions validated with Alphalens. No dimension with consistently negative IC remains active.

product_roadmap.goal: Transition from paper trading to live trading on a single IBKR account with strict risk controls and full observability.
product_roadmap.rationale: Live trading introduces real execution risk, slippage, and psychological pressure. The system must have proven paper profitability (Phase 1), upgraded signal intelligence (Phase 2), and robust risk controls before real money is at stake. This phase maps to the live_trading_single_account stage.
features: Correlation-Aware Position Management (B P2) — 20-day rolling correlation matrix
Intra-Portfolio Rolling Correlation Guard with Sector Cap (B P2)
Portfolio Heat Monitor with Correlation-Adjusted Exposure Cap (B P2)
Dynamic Volatility-Based Position Sizing (C P2) — ATR ceiling on Kelly output
Rolling CVaR Dashboard Metric and Per-Account Risk Budget (C P2)
HMM Regime Detection (D P2) — 3-state HMM on vol + RSI
Regime-Conditioned Position Size Scalar (D P2) — connect HMM to position sizing
Triple Barrier Method for ML Training Labels (B P2)
WebSocket Signal Broadcaster (B P1) — brain publishes signals in real time
Per-Account Local Dashboard (MA1 P1) — live positions, P&L, trade history on port 8080
Telegram Bot — Notifications and Quick Commands (C P2) — trade notifications and morning brief
Live trading go-live checklist: max position size caps, daily loss limit, manual kill switch confirmed
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Live trading running for 30+ days with positive P&L, no critical execution bugs, drawdown brake triggered and respected at least once, all risk controls verified with real orders.

product_roadmap.goal: Move the live trading system to a cloud VPS so it runs 24/7 without depending on a local machine.
product_roadmap.rationale: Cloud hosting is a prerequisite for multi-user: the system must be stable as a hosted service before multiple users connect. This phase maps to cloud_hosted_single_account. Containerisation and reconnection logic are foundational for everything that follows.
features: Cloud VPS Setup and Deployment (MA2 P1) — Ubuntu 22, Docker, shared brain container
Reconnection Logic — Backoff, Sequence, State Sync (MA2 P1) — resilient container-to-brain connections
Per-User Docker Compose Containers (MA2 P1) — isolated containers with encrypted credentials
Signal Registry — Per-User Favourite Symbols (MA2 P1)
Connection Manager — Multi-Account IBKR (MA1 P0) — manage multiple IB() instances
AccountDecisionAgent Class (MA1 P0) — per-account agent downstream of 6-agent pipeline
Per-Account Config Profiles (MA1 P0) — account-aware profile system replacing flat CONFIG
Parameterise account_id Through Orders and Risk (MA1 P0) — audit all direct CONFIG reads
Extract Signal Objects from Brain Loop (MA1 P0) — if not already done in Phase 1
Monitoring: uptime alerting, container health checks, automatic restart on failure
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: System runs unattended on cloud VPS for 7+ days without manual intervention, survives at least one network interruption with automatic reconnect, all single-account live trading functionality confirmed working in cloud environment.

product_roadmap.goal: Onboard a small group of friends and family (2-5 users) who connect their own IBKR accounts through a web interface.
product_roadmap.rationale: This is the multi_user_beta stage. All prior infrastructure must be proven before real users with real money are added. Focus is on onboarding flow, per-user isolation, authentication, and observability — not on adding new signal features.
features: Per-User Dashboard Authentication (MA3 P1) — login, sessions, credential isolation
One-Time User Setup Screen (MA3 P1) — guided onboarding: IBKR credentials, risk profile selection
Web Dashboard — Live Positions and P&L (MA3 P1) — browser-accessible per-user dashboard
Per-Account Risk Profile Config Schema with Multi-Tier Presets (B P2) — conservative/moderate/aggressive presets
Per-Account Risk Limits (E P1) — independent max drawdown, position size, sector concentration per user
Account-Aware Order Router (E P1) — route orders to correct IBKR account
Cross-Account Correlation Guard (E P2) — detect correlated exposure across accounts
Unified Trade Journal (E P2) — cross-account trade log with account tags
Account Performance Comparison Dashboard (E P2) — side-by-side Sharpe, drawdown, P&L
Telegram Bot per user (MA3 P2) — per-user trade notifications
Reddit Mention Velocity Dimension (B P2) — if research validates it adds alpha by this stage
Beta user feedback collection mechanism
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: 2-5 beta users successfully onboarded, each running independently with their own IBKR accounts, no cross-account data leakage detected, all users able to view their own P&L and adjust risk settings without technical assistance.

product_roadmap.goal: Harden the platform for broader use: security, scalability, compliance, and operational excellence.
product_roadmap.rationale: This is the multi_user_production stage. Beta feedback will reveal the gaps. Production requires higher reliability guarantees, proper security auditing, and operational procedures that don't depend on the founder being available.
features: Security audit: credential storage, API key rotation, session management
Scalability testing: confirm architecture supports 10+ concurrent users
Walk-Forward Weight Calibration (D P2) — automated signal weight optimisation
HMM + regime system fully integrated and validated in production
Operational runbooks: incident response, user offboarding, account suspension
Billing / access control layer if monetisation is planned
Full documentation for end users: setup guide, risk explainer, FAQ
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Platform running with 5+ active users for 60+ days, no security incidents, founder can take a week off without the system failing, all operational procedures documented.

immediate_next_action.action: Diagnose and fix the test suite reporting 0 passed / 0 failed / 0 errors, and restore all tests to a confirmed passing state.
immediate_next_action.rationale: A test suite reporting zero results is a silent catastrophe — it means every recent commit, including the three critical capital-safety bug fixes and the Phase E multi-account work, has been merged without any automated validation. With 942 tests referenced in recent commits and 32 test files, this is almost certainly a pytest collection or runner failure. Until this is fixed, there is no way to know whether the system is working, and no feature built on top of it can be trusted. This is the highest-severity risk in the project and blocks everything else.
immediate_next_action.cowork_prompt: We need to urgently fix the Decifer test suite. It is currently reporting 0 passed / 0 failed / 0 errors — which means pytest is failing to collect or run tests at all, not that tests are passing. Recent commits reference 942 tests across 32 test files. Please: (1) Run pytest with -v and --tb=short and share the full output so we can see the collection error. (2) Identify the root cause — likely a conftest.py import failure, a missing fixture, a module-level import error in one of the 26 source modules, or a pytest configuration issue. (3) Fix the collection failure. (4) Work through any remaining test failures until we have a confirmed green suite or a documented list of known failures with explicit reasons. Do not move on to any feature work until we have a trustworthy test suite.

untracked_ideas.title: Manual Kill Switch / Emergency Stop
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by vision (live trading with real money for friends and family) and risk management best practices
untracked_ideas.rationale: There is no mention anywhere in the feature pipeline of a one-button emergency stop that closes all positions and halts the bot. This is a non-negotiable safety feature before any real money is involved, and it must be accessible via the web dashboard and ideally via Telegram.

untracked_ideas.title: User Onboarding Documentation / Risk Disclosure
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by vision (friends and family connecting their own broker accounts)
untracked_ideas.rationale: Non-technical users connecting real IBKR accounts to an automated trading bot need clear written explanation of risks, what the bot does, what it cannot guarantee, and how to disconnect. This is both a legal/ethical necessity and a trust-building requirement. No spec for this exists anywhere.

untracked_ideas.title: Paper Trading P&L Dashboard (Pre-Live Validation)
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by current stage (paper_trading_single_account) and the exit criteria for Phase 1
untracked_ideas.rationale: There is a web dashboard feature in the pipeline but it appears scoped for live trading. There is no explicit spec for a paper trading P&L tracker that shows cumulative returns, per-trade outcomes, and win rate over the paper trading observation period. This is needed to actually validate Phase 1 exit criteria.

untracked_ideas.title: Deduplication Audit Across Active and Backlog Feature Lists
untracked_ideas.source: Observed directly in feature pipeline data — Per-Account Config Profiles, AccountDecisionAgent, Connection Manager, Reconnection Logic, WebSocket Signal Broadcaster, Cloud VPS, Per-User Docker Compose, Per-Account Local Dashboard, Telegram Bot, Web Dashboard all appear in both Active and Backlog with different spec text
untracked_ideas.rationale: Having the same feature in both Active and Backlog with different descriptions creates ambiguity about which spec is authoritative, whether work is double-counted, and what the true state of implementation is. A deduplication pass is needed to make the pipeline trustworthy as a planning tool.

untracked_ideas.title: Daily Loss Limit / Per-Session Risk Cap
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by fractional Kelly research findings and live trading risk management best practices
untracked_ideas.rationale: The drawdown brake is tiered but appears to operate on equity curve drawdown. A separate per-day maximum loss cap (e.g. halt trading if daily P&L drops below -2%) is a standard professional risk control that prevents a bad morning from becoming a catastrophic day. Not explicitly specced anywhere.

untracked_ideas.title: IBKR Paper Trading vs Live Account Toggle
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by the staged roadmap (paper → live) and the multi-account infrastructure
untracked_ideas.rationale: As the system moves from paper to live, users need a clear, auditable way to know which mode they are in. There is no spec for a configuration toggle or UI indicator that shows whether orders are being routed to paper or live, and prevents accidental live orders during testing.

risks.risk: Test suite reporting 0 passed / 0 failed / 0 errors — silent validation failure means all recent code including critical capital-safety bug fixes is unverified
risks.mitigation: Make test suite restoration the single focus of the next session. Do not merge any further feature work until pytest reports a concrete, trustworthy result. Add CI enforcement to block future merges without passing tests.

risks.risk: Feature pipeline is mis-sequenced — multi-account infrastructure (Phase E/MA1/MA2) is being built in parallel with paper trading validation, adding complexity before the core is proven
risks.mitigation: Freeze multi-account and cloud infrastructure work until Phase 1 (paper trading hardening) exit criteria are met. Reassign active development focus to signal quality, risk controls, and paper trading observability.

risks.risk: Duplicate features across Active and Backlog lists (at least 10 features appear in both with different specs) — creates planning confusion and risk of double implementation or spec drift
risks.mitigation: Conduct a deduplication audit of the feature pipeline. For each duplicate, identify the authoritative spec, archive the other, and confirm implementation status. This should be done before Phase 1 planning is locked.

risks.risk: 50 active features with no clear priority sequencing — the sheer volume risks diluting engineering focus and shipping nothing to completion
risks.mitigation: Adopt a strict WIP limit: no more than 3-5 active features in flight at any time. Use the roadmap phases to sequence features, and only pull new features into active when current ones are shipped and tested.

risks.risk: No explicit paper trading profitability gate before live trading — the roadmap stages exist but there is no documented decision criteria for when to advance from paper to live
risks.mitigation: Define and document explicit numeric exit criteria for Phase 1: minimum trading days, minimum Sharpe, maximum drawdown tolerance, win rate floor. These should be agreed before live trading is considered.

risks.risk: Agents JSON parse error in production logs — agents.py is failing to parse LLM responses and falling back silently, potentially degrading signal quality without visibility
risks.mitigation: Audit agents.py error handling for JSON parse failures. Add explicit alerting when agents fail to parse (not just logging). Implement a retry or fallback strategy. Track agent failure rate as a monitoring metric.

risks.risk: bot.py at 2,787 lines — largest module is likely doing too much, making it fragile and hard to test
risks.mitigation: The Extract Signal Objects from Brain Loop feature (already in pipeline) addresses part of this. Prioritise this refactor in Phase 1 to make bot.py testable and reduce the risk of regression during signal and risk changes.

risks.risk: No mention of a manual kill switch or emergency stop anywhere in the feature pipeline — live trading with real money for non-technical users without a kill switch is unacceptable
risks.mitigation: Add an emergency stop feature to the Phase 3 backlog immediately. It must close all open positions, cancel all pending orders, and halt the bot. Accessible via web dashboard and Telegram. This is a gate condition for live trading.

risks.risk: Reddit Mention Velocity dimension has mixed research evidence (acknowledged in the feature spec itself) but is still in the active pipeline — risk of adding noise rather than alpha
risks.mitigation: Gate the Reddit dimension behind Alphalens IC validation before it is allowed to influence live signals. If IC is not consistently positive in walk-forward validation, exclude it. Do not include it in Phase 1 or 2 without validation.

risks.risk: Friends and family users connecting real IBKR accounts — no mention of user agreements, risk disclosures, or legal considerations anywhere in the pipeline
risks.mitigation: Add a user onboarding documentation and risk disclosure task to the Phase 5 backlog. At minimum, users should sign off on understanding that the bot is experimental and that losses are possible before their accounts are connected.

roadmap_gaps: The following capabilities are implied by the vision but not explicitly specced in any feature or backlog item: (1) Emergency kill switch / manual halt — no feature exists for one-button position closure and bot halt, which is essential for live trading with non-technical users; (2) Paper trading P&L tracker — there is no explicit spec for tracking and displaying paper trading performance over the observation period needed to validate Phase 1 exit criteria; (3) IBKR paper vs live account toggle with UI indicator — the transition from paper to live needs an explicit, auditable mode switch; (4) Per-day maximum loss cap / session risk limit — the drawdown brake operates on equity curve drawdown but a daily loss halt is a separate and necessary control; (5) User risk disclosure and onboarding documentation — non-technical users connecting real accounts need written risk explanation; (6) Feature pipeline deduplication — at least 10 features appear in both Active and Backlog with different specs, creating an unresolved ambiguity about what is planned and what is built; (7) CI/CD pipeline — no mention of automated test enforcement on merge, which is why the test suite failure may have gone undetected; (8) Operational runbooks and incident response — no spec for what happens when the bot crashes, loses connection, or makes an unexpected trade at 2am; (9) Backtesting result storage and comparison — walk-forward backtesting is specced but there is no mention of how results are stored, compared across runs, or used to make go/no-go decisions; (10) Agent performance tracking — agents.py is failing to parse JSON in production but there is no monitoring feature specced for tracking per-agent recommendation quality or failure rates over time.
